who carries the nuclear football for the president The term "nuclear football" refers to a portable briefcase containing the secure second-strike launch authorization codes for U.S. nuclear weapons. This crucial responsibility is carried out by members of the military serving as bodyguards, ensuring the safety and security of these codes even when the president is not in their official quarters or at a fixed command center.

This bodyguard plays a critical role in safeguarding the weapon release authority associated with the football.
